steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The first sentence states the general aim: analyze "diet composition of cattle." The second sentence narrows that to a specific detail: the ratio of three plant subtypes. The transition must signal that the second sentence is a particular instance of the first — a narrowing from general to specific.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Instead,’: Signals contrast or replacement. The second sentence does not contradict or replace the first — it refines it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘All the same,’: Signals concession or continuation despite something. No concession is present here. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Therefore,’: Signals a conclusion drawn from evidence. The second sentence is not a result of the first — it specifies what the first meant. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Specifically,’: Signals that what follows is a particular detail elaborating the preceding general statement — exactly the general-to-specific narrowing the passage requires. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
